reviewTitle: Beware of the hidden money grabs
reviewDescription: Beware of the hidden money grabs. Outdoorsy advertises affordable rates, but their checkout process is loaded with undisclosed service fees, ambiguous platform charges, and forced insurance add-ons. They do everything they can to inflate the final price. Very disappointed by the lack of pricing transparency. I had originally booked and was paying $217/day (I calculated this myself by taking the full price I paid and divided over the days). When I added one more day to the trip a week later, they charged me $263 for the extra day. When I contacted the customer service team they couldn’t explain the reason for the increased fees. They said the same generic “taxes, service fees, and insurance” but couldn’t break down or show me how the service fees had increased $46 for adding a single day, even when accounting for the insurance and sales tax. And you can’t pay the host directly because they force you to go through their platform so they can collect the service fees. I reached out to the host directly and he didn’t mention raising his daily rate but said I had to go through the website. I understand the business model but if you want repeat customers you need to earn trust… and Outdoorsy has failed to do that.
reviewCompanyResponse:
Hi Chuck!

Thank you for taking the time to share your perspective with us. 

Please allow me to clarify that Outdoorsy operates as a peer-to-peer marketplace platform. This means that, aside from our standard 10% service fee, hosts independently determine nightly rates and additional fees associated with their listings. 

While we are unable to authenticate the specific experience described based on the information you provided to this platform, generally speaking, nightly rate fluctuations can sometimes occur due to host-configured pricing settings, such as weekend pricing, holiday rates, seasonal adjustments, or other automated pricing factors.

Transparency is vitally important to us, which is why Outdoorsy provides a full cost breakdown prior to booking confirmation. This includes nightly rates, guest fees, optional host add-ons, insurance protection costs, taxes, applicable service fees, etc., so guests can review the complete pricing details and ensure they are fully informed of the total reservation cost before completing their booking.

We encourage you to contact our support team directly and verify your account information so we may review the reservation details more thoroughly and provide additional clarification specific to your booking.

Thanks again for taking a moment to share your feedback! It's greatly appreciated.
 
Outdoorsy Support

reviewTitle: Well, here's what happened to me
reviewDescription: Well, somebody should have told me how to actually complete the rental. I chose an RV, input my dates, received a cost summary but couldn't find a way to actually make the deal. Tried dealing with the WORST AI experience I've ever had (and AI is ra rarely a good experience but dont get me started). After an eternity of trying to make the deal, I was offered a phone number. I called it and met a nice guy who helped me navigate the peculiar procedure used by this company. I had to "register" but was never told this on the website; I was directed to messaging the owner who never messaged me back. I put my phone number in the message bc I wanted to arrange to SEE the vehicle in person before I committed 4000 dollars. Two things: at this point I was creating a process based on many experiences of buying and renting stuff on the internet and doing it without any direction from Outdoorsy. Well, somehow I failed.  Then, I got an email: "you haven't completed rental...."  Against my better judgment, I took the bait. I was offered AI disguised with a human name. Finally, I know what AI is going to do; it's going to make murderers of us all.  Now, after hours of effort, I still have my money but no RV.  Anyone out there willing to rent an RV to a retired attorney with the highest credit rating available, money to burn,and passionately wanting to experience RVing for a couple of weeks?  I'm your guy.
reviewCompanyResponse:
Hi there,

I'm very sorry to hear you had trouble completing your reservation and that your experience with our chat service did not provide the level of support you were looking for. While our AI is designed to handle quick requests, it is certainly not meant to replace human support. If you find that the AI is not meeting your needs, simply type "live agent" or "representative" in the chat, and you will be connected to a specialist immediately.

Additionally, I'd like to reassure you that our 24/7 Customer Support number, 877-723-7232, is clearly indicated on every page of our website to ensure you can always reach our live support team directly.

I'd love the opportunity to learn more about your experience; however, I'm unable to locate your account with the information available here. Please contact me directly at experience@outdoorsy.com with "ATTN: DALE" in the subject line, and I'll be more than happy to assist you with your booking personally.

reviewTitle: DO NOT RENT YOUR RIGG WITH OUTDOORSY
reviewDescription:
I was a host for Outdoorsy for 3.5 years, with a 5 Star rating and used to love providing an experience for renters, with discounts and free add-ons.  I have had a lot inexperienced renters in the past, and most which have damaged my RV.   Outdoorsy agreed to pay for damages and put my RV back to it's original state, but each incident Outdoorsy/Roamly paid less and less of the expenses, and in the end realized that I have had to over $6000 in damages to my RV. Replaced my awning twice, new water heater, new door handles, CO2 unit, table, generator, back support bar, ladder, and the list goes on, due to lack of proof a renter caused the damage.  If I could suggest to anyone, do not rent your RV with Outdoorsy and be weary renter of  all the hidden fees, it does not go to the host, the host only get less than 50% of what you have to pay. 

So when a renter hit a Gator and took off the side of my door, and only paid for the replacement and not the paint, which I had to pay out of pocket.  OR when a renter ran into a tree and took off my awning and damaged the back of the RV and it was said to be  two different claims and Outdoorsy only paid for the awning and not the back of the RV, which I had to pay for.  OR this last time when my RV was hit so hard that the door to the Generator was torn off and messed up my Adjuster Arm and Alignment, relocated my generator, but Outdoorsy would not pay for that either, and I had to come out of pocket once again.  The last time was just the last time, not the many other times.  Like when my renter busted my water heater or another one yanked off my door handle.  I can name plenty more, but for the sake of space I digress. DO NOT RENT YOUR RV WITH OUTDOORSY!!!!
reviewCompanyResponse:
Hi Charese,

We're sorry to hear that you're dissatisfied with the payment for your claim. Thank you for sharing your experience and allowing us the opportunity to clarify the matter further. Upon closer review of the adjuster's report, it appears the invoice submitted for the supplement request does not provide evidence of impact damage or confirm that the failure resulted from the rental incident.

Insurance coverage is strictly limited to the specific damages that occurred during the booking in question. If you have any further questions or need clarification, please feel free to reach back out to your adjuster directly within the email thread where you've been communicating.

Outdoorsy Support

reviewCompanyResponse:
Hi Bill,

Thank you for taking the time to share your experience and allow us the opportunity to clarify the security deposit authorization hold process, as previously explained by our customer support team.

We can confirm that only one authorization hold in the amount of $1,500 was placed on your credit card. This hold was not a charge. It was an authorization to temporarily reserve funds and, as designed, the same authorization amount was reauthorized seven days later. This does not constitute a second charge, as no charge occurs during an authorization hold unless the host submits a valid claim.

Authorization holds automatically expire if no claims are submitted for damages, ancillary fees, or overages. Because these holds are not charges, the funds do not appear as a credit on your account when released. Instead, the hold simply expires and the funds become available to you again.

In this instance, the host claimed a small portion of the security deposit for ancillary dumping charges, which were disclosed in the listing and agreed to at the time of booking. The remaining balance of your security deposit was released successfully on August 30, 2025.

If you require confirmation of when the authorization fully cleared on your account, we recommend contacting your credit card issuer directly, as processing timelines vary by financial institution. Thanks again for your perspective.

Outdoorsy Support

Outdoorsy claims to "secure a deposit", however they will only "secure" it for 7 days from the time of the credit card hold. However, if the rental or claim goes beyond that 7 day period, then they won't cover it. They never told me this. In fact, when the renter brought back the unit late, not dumped, excessively dirty, damaged, and missing jacks, I placed a claim and was told the 7 days would be pushed out to 28 days so I could assess damage. I assessed damages and then was told they could no longer secure the deposit. This is fraudulent and I hope we can ban together to hold Outdoorsy responsible for breach of contract, negligence, and misrepresentation
reviewCompanyResponse:
Hi Stephanie,

Thank you for taking a moment to share your feedback and giving us the chance to clear things up about how our security deposit authorization holds work. The authorization renews itself every seven days by default, unless it’s claimed at the end of the trip or seven days have passed after the trip with no claim. At that point, the hold simply drops off and is released back to the guest.

If a 30-day hold was placed, that timeline overrides the seven-day renewals. In your case, because a chargeback was filed by the guest with their financial institution, the bank automatically declined the reauthorization, which caused the hold to release. That part of the process is fully controlled by the bank, not us.

We truly appreciate you sharing your perspective and giving us the opportunity to clarify this process, as our support team and management reviewed with you earlier.

Outdoorsy Support

I would also allow a spot for the host to put their phone number so guest can call and speak to the person that they will be renting from vs. text/email only, which can really drag out the conversation. Many times it is much more convenient to make a phone call and to discuss what to expect or how to make things easier for both the host & guest instead of relying only on messaging through the app.

reviewTitle: Do better and be less greedy
reviewDescription: The outdoorsy host app has continuously gotten more difficult to use. Pictures can no longer be uploaded, they must be taken at the time of handoff, the messaging functionality is atrocious (difficult to use through the app, I typically have to use a PC to message people back which reduces my response time), and the calendar/editing features are difficult and often not full featured like the web-based site. It is annoying that outdoorsy takes such a large cut for providing very little to hosts. Also, they purchased a large RV park near me that used to allow me to dump my RV there and Outdoorsy no longer allows public dumping (even for a fee), very annoying since it is one of a few places that exist in my area to dump. This company has gotten greedier since I started listing my RV with them and provides very little host benefits.

The host fees are out of control, they take over 20% even if I charge the renter to replace something after the rental or to conduct a service (like a lost fork, or a dump fee, or my service of delivery). But here is the worst, a recent rental had a major blowout when the renter was towing, causing about $1800 in damage. Not only did outdoorsy not accept their "excess supplemental" claim after the renter was denied by their own insurance, nor my damage claim, they also released the security deposit back to the renter. So I am stuck with having to reach out and beg the renter to cover some of the cost. Your trailers are at risk, your business is at risk, don't listen to social media, it is not worth it.

reviewDescription: Overall the app is easy to use the but the key exchange process is confusing to new users without any rundown of how it works.

The system is great and using the website and app quite easy. As hosts our needs were met and our guests needs were met so that makes us happy. The only thing we had a hard time with is that we had to call your customer service number twice and both times the people on the phone were kind of condescending. It kind of made us worried about needing to call in the future.

Most of our bookings have come through this platform, so I’ve been putting extra time and care into refining our listing here. The customer service team has been responsive and helpful whenever I’ve had questions, and guests seem to trust the Outdoorsy platform, which gives me confidence as a host.

I do wish the service fees weren’t taken from the delivery and cleaning portions since those are owner-provided services, not part of Outdoorsy’s offering. It would make more sense if fees were only deducted from the booking itself. Still, I understand that the platform provides valuable peace of mind, insurance coverage, and customer support.

Overall, I’m grateful for the opportunity to connect with great renters through this service and plan to continue using it as we grow our own client base.

reviewTitle: Very good service, but needs a few tweaks to be great.
reviewDescription: Outdoorsy has a very good platform for helping camper-owners rent out their campers when not in use. The website is great, the app needs work. The only other complaint I have is the paperwork; Outdoorsy requests the host to print out a plethora of documents for both the host and the guest to sign/initial. This should all be done digitally. reviewDescription: always a pleasure working with outdoorsy

I hadn’t notice the insurance charge until after signing up.  I attempted to have that removed and at the same time read reviews of the company which prompted me to cancel.  The cancellation fee was 25%.  The rental fee was $340 so to me the fee should be $85, but no they count the prep fee and insurance in that cancellation.  This is not a company I want anything to do with or will recommend.
reviewCompanyResponse:
Hi Geoff,

Thank you so much for your feedback. Please allow me to clarify that Outdoorsy operates as a peer-to-peer platform. This means that aside from our standard 10% service fee, all other fees are set directly by the host, including their chosen cancellation policy, as we offer three different tiers to suit various preferences.

We also provide three insurance protection packages designed to accommodate different budget and coverage needs, as well as stationary coverage options for rentals that are delivered and not intended to be moved during the trip. Removal of Outdoorsy’s insurance protection after a booking is confirmed is possible, provided the host agrees to accept the guest’s personal carrier in lieu of our protection package.

Thank you again for taking the time to share your perspective; we truly appreciate it.

Donna
Outdoorsy Support

reviewTitle: Renting from outdoorsy will put your safety at risk
reviewDescription:
Rented a sprinter from a well rated host in oregon. Host was strange, but thorough and seemed to take very good care of the rv.  Only downside that the host pointed out was the older tires with worn tread, but he assured us for our trip that it would be fine. He was getting them replaced next week.  Tires were polished, and pressure was ok. I trusted their opinion of the state of the vehicle.

45mins on the highway, the left rear tire had an extreme tire shoulder wall failure at 60mph on a highway. The whole vehicle swerved, nearly tipped, but I regained control and brought it to the side.  Could have been seriously injured. My family was in the back seat.

Deeper inspection of the tires showed the surviving tires shoulder wall tread was at/below the legal road limit, cracked, and risking further blowout.  The blown tire was completely separated from it's treads. During the separation, the treads damaged the body of the sprinter. I swapped the spare, and took it to a tire center for further evaluation.  Expert there said all tires risked further blowout, they need immediate replacement, but you should be able to get back home if you're careful.

Took detailed photos, documented everything, returned the vehicle cleaned the night of.  Outdoorsy claims rejected coverage of repairs since it was a non collision blowout.  Outdoorsy resolution team sided with the Host.  They claimed that after I accepted the vehicle, tires are the renters responsibility.  None of the photos or expert opinions were considered.  It does not matter if the host was negligent.  So, the host took our 1k security deposit, giving him free tires, while my family's life was put at risk.  He stated he "wouldn't pursue this further", which hurt since he put my family's safety at risk to save money.

I'm being punished since I didn't catch this during inspection. The fact that the host did not provide a road legal vehicle for rental wasn't considered by the dispute resolution team.

Here is what I've learned from discussions with outdoorsy's dispute resolution team:

1. Outdoorsy is not in the business of ensuring a safe rental experience, rather they want to ensure an adequate supply of happy Hosts for rentals.
2. Safety is exclusively up to the Renter to determine during the short inspection period with the Host.
3. The Host is only responsible for clearly visible maintenance or safety concerns. This is in conflict with Outdoorsy own terms of service, but they will side with the Host since it's in their business best interest. Safety responsibility only applies to clearly identifiable issues during inspection. By extension, anything non-visible is the renter's responsibility. 
4. The Host will not be found liable by Outdoorsy for any issue resulting from the lack of required maintenance that can't be obviously found during the short inspection period.  Regardless of required maintenance and responsibilities clearly called out by Outdoorsy's terms of service (> 50% tire tread).
5. Any damages caused by the lack of maintenance during the booking period will be the responsibility of the renter, at the renter's cost to safety and expense, regardless of root cause.

Outdoorsy can and should do better.  

-Patrick N
reviewCompanyResponse:
Hi Patrick,

Please accept my sincere apologies for your negative experience with our dispute resolution process. This is certainly not the outcome we want our guests to have, and I appreciate you taking the time to share your feedback. At Outdoorsy, safety is paramount, and we are committed to ensuring that every dispute is handled through a fair and impartial resolution process. While I’m pleased that a significant courtesy refund was issued from Outdoorsy to help offset the costs of the RV damage repairs, I also want to reassure you that our policies and procedures are designed with the best interests of both guests and hosts in mind. To help avoid future misunderstandings, we strongly encourage all guests to carefully review our full Terms and Conditions—available on our website, in the rental agreement, and within each Outdoorsy dashboard—prior to confirming a reservation. In addition, guests are strongly encouraged to take detailed pre-trip photos to provide clear documentation. Hosts, meanwhile, are required to maintain and provide a recent safety inspection of their RV. These inspections include critical safety checks such as tire integrity, tread depth, and brake function. Each inspection must be submitted to the resolution specialist and is considered official evidence in the event of a dispute. I can confirm that the inspection report for the RV you rented was utilized to determine the final resolution decision. Together, these steps help ensure a safe, transparent, and fair experience for all. Thank you again for sharing your experience with us.

Donna
Outdoorsy Support

reviewTitle: We booked a rental and had to cancel
reviewDescription:
We booked an RV rental in New Haven, IN through Outdoorsy in August 2025. While the initial booking process went smoothly, we encountered several concerning issues with the host.
The host repeatedly attempted to establish direct contact outside of the platform and sent unsolicited social media friend requests, which violates Outdoorsy's policies. This made us uncomfortable and raised red flags about professionalism. The day before our scheduled delivery, the host claimed we had selected the incorrect delivery option and suggested we were at fault. He recommended canceling the booking, stating he "should" be able to accept a new reservation. Given the timing and the host's expressed unavailability, we felt we had no choice but to cancel our rental the night before departure.

Fortunately, we were able to secure a rental from an established RV dealer. This experience highlighted the advantages of working with professional dealers who provide proper equipment, safety gear, and reliable service.  This included correct hitching equipment and electrical connections.

While Outdoorsy may work well for some RV owners looking to rent out their vehicles, our experience suggests potential safety and reliability concerns when dealing with individual owners versus established dealers. For peace of mind and safety assurance, we recommend considering professional RV rental companies for your next trip.

reviewTitle: I proved I was scammed.. here’s what happened..
reviewDescription:
I would give it 0. To begin, I was naive and trusting enough to not back myself up by taking photos of my rental.. turns out Outdoorsy’s platform and negligent customer service make it very easy for the Host’s to scam their guests. I didn’t even need photo proof to prove it. 

I rented a camper for one week. Th host claimed the backwater tank was faulty when I noticed the backwater was half full.. I believed her. For perspective, a Thor class C holds 30 gallons of black water.. which means a half full tank is 15 gallons (or about 57 litres). I specifically told my group, no pooping in camper. The public bathroom is just a few dozen steps away. “No poop!”, I said.. well the camper smelt like poop for 6 days and the ride home was like driving through a sewer.

Now there was a bunch of complaints from the host about their peace of junk camper. I broke a lot of stuff apparently. They charged me for breaking a super glued countertop. I don’t know about ya’ll. But a SUPER GLUED repair, is not a repair. Matter a fact it’s not damage. After dozens of back and forth emails, I finally get picture proof of the broken counter. The host legit, ripped it off the countertop and sent that as their claim. I sent them a picture of the camper ad with the super glued camper clear as day right there. These hosts clearly have this counter broken every single rental. Because we’re all stupid right?

A mud flap I damaged from getting into a pot hole, forced the mud flap onto the tire and it scraped down to nothing. These mud flaps are about $30-$40 to repair. The host charged me $487 for it. I finally get a receipt of it from outdoorsy and guess what? It’s a ford mud flap that is nowhere close to the one I damaged. In fact it’s the most fanciest ford mud flap you’ve ever seen. Here’s the kicker.. it’s a mud flap for a ford F150, F250 or F350. Easy scam. The host reams me up the hoo-ha by ordering $437 mud flap off Amazon, proving the cost with an Amazon receipt, and then return it for a full refund. If you subtract $40 from $437 that is $397. That’s an easy profit for a scam. 

Outdoorsy offered me $500 for this experience. Don’t they care that I’m offended by being scammed? Don’t they care how it makes them look when they allow a host to get away with this? Even if they ban the host, did they make me as a customer feel safe, happy and trusting? Absolutely not. I was mad at the host for scamming me. When the Outdoorsy support made me feel like I was cared for, I felt relieved. But now I have a different sentiment about the whole thing.. How can anyone just stand by and allow a customer to get scammed? They let me get scammed. Dissatisfied, disappointed and this isn’t the last place they’ll find me writing a review. 

Update: the responder to this review confirms outdoorsy closed my case without telling me so. They did not care to know if I was satisfied or not. They clearly won’t bother to entertain the evidence I put forward. At this point I’m a nuisance. They refunded me $500 but it doesn’t fix the fact I was ripped off.

reviewTitle: I would give zero stars if I could
reviewDescription:
I would give zero stars if I could. I booked an RV through Outdoorsy six months in advance for Burning Man, which was supposed to be my 30th birthday trip. Just days before departure, the host canceled on me. Outdoorsy’s response? A refund and nothing more.
That does absolutely nothing to fix the fact that, by then, every other affordable option was long gone. After months of planning and thousands of dollars invested in supplies, I had to scramble all day and am now taking a box truck instead of the RV I paid for. The entire experience I planned is ruined.
Outdoorsy loves to market itself as a “trusted” platform, but when hosts cancel, renters are left stranded with no backup options, no protection, and no accountability. If you’re planning a big trip or festival, I urge you to avoid Outdoorsy — they cannot be relied on. I’ll be making sure the Burning Man and festival community knows this too.

reviewTitle: OUTDOORSY NIGHTMARE! Stay away from Outdoorsy!!
reviewDescription:
We're appalled by Outdoorsy’s gross negligence and won't utilize them in the future. Outdoorsy doesn't vet the owners and their vehicles before renting them out. Outdoorsy is grossly negligent, failing to exercise the degree of care a reasonably prudent company would exercise under the same circumstances and this negligence can result in harm, injury and even death. Outdoorsy breached their duty of care that could cause foreseeable damage. We could have been killed as the vehicle rented on Outdoorsy was a death trap. 

To accommodate 8, I rented a luxury sprinter. believing it would be safe and reliable. After driving three hours, we stopped to purchase gas and noticed the front bumper was falling off. It had been secured with duct tape. Then, we noticed steel coming out of the left front tire. Fortunately, we were close to a tire company who said we were minutes away from a blow out which would've caused a major accident. This delay caused us to be 3 hours late for our event.

The longer we drove, the less the brakes worked.  We had to push them farther and farther towards the floor to stop.  The bathroom vent was broken and when it rained, water poured in, ruining our clothes. 

Outdoorsy refused to compensate us in any way for their gross negligence in renting us the unsafe vehicle they advertised as safe for travel.  Our trip was an absolute nightmare!

Needless to say we planned a trip that involved 2000 miles over the allowed limit and “our mistake” was to assume the standard going rate for the mileage overage fee. After our credit card declined when we got charged the overage fee we found out that instead of the planned extra $1000 we were being charged $5000. Never would have planned a site seeing trip like this with a $2.50 a mile overage. Absolutely ridiculous.

Edit: Went through booking on my phone again just to be sure I wasn’t providing false information. I was not. There is no way to see the overage charge on a mobile device until after you book. Then if you don’t read the final booking carefully you won’t realize that it is 5 times the going rate until after you are already home and they charge you.

Been getting the run around with Outdoorsy customer service. They are the worst. They keep saying they are not to blame, but obviously they figured out that they were because they changed their website so they can retroactively claim they are not at fault. They want us to deal with the owner of the RV instead of them to negotiate with him when it is their fault. Don’t believe a word of their reply to this post because they are that dishonest.

All I can say is they are definitely committed to deception and wish to always push the blame back on the customer. It is obvious they could care less about making the situation ethically right and are really only looking to blame the customer for not combing their site for information that was not there until after being called out on it. Avoid them at all costs.

Needless to say the customer “support” team is a joke and has no authority to do anything except make up excuses and lame apologies that do not admit any fault. reviewTitle: If you're a host considering Outdoorsy
reviewDescription:
If you're a host considering Outdoorsy, proceed with caution. I had an account previously and decided to return—big mistake. I was prepared to resubmit everything from scratch, which would’ve been fine. But because my original email was no longer valid, I couldn’t reset my password or create a new setup with that email. That took a full day to resolve.

Then came the real headache. After spending two hours setting up my listing again, I hit a wall with the W-9 form—it wouldn’t submit. That issue still isn’t fixed after 11 days, despite two emails claiming it was. I’ve spent hours emailing, calling, uploading print screens, and getting nowhere.

Customer service is polite but powerless. You can’t speak to anyone who can actually fix the problem. Everything is “escalated” to the technical or tax team, but no one follows up. Apparently, the tax team doesn’t work weekends… and I'm beginning to wonder if they work during the week either. Customer service reps even admit they’re frustrated by the escalation delays.

Outdoorsy's site claims a 24–48 hour response window, but that seems more like wishful thinking. I haven’t heard a thing in four days.

It’s now June in Maine—peak RV rental season—and I’m losing valuable time and income because of their broken system. I've switched to RVShare out of desperation. I’m not sure if it’s much better, but I can't waste another minute dealing with Outdoorsy's dysfunction.

For a platform that takes a 25% cut, they ought to provide actual support. Sadly, I’ve since found I’m not the only one with these issues—if you look up reviews, you’ll see plenty of similar stories. Outdoorsy really needs to get its act together.
reviewCompanyResponse:
Hi Darlene,

I'm very sorry to hear you've had a less-than-perfect experience with our service due to the delay in resolving the issue with your tax information on your Outdoorsy account. I know how important it is for you to get this resolved immediately, and we definitely don't want you missing out on potential bookings for the peak RV rental season, so I'd like to reassure you that we've followed up with our Tax team and made your case a priority. I'll personally get in touch with you soon regarding this. Thank you for taking a moment to provide us with your feedback. 

Dale R.
Outdoorsy Support

reviewDescription: Overall outdoorsy has been very user friendly.  As someone renting out my daily driver, a camper van, I’d say I have to probe for more info than what outdoorsy requests. Where they are going and how many people will be traveling are a couple that would be very useful.

Disappointing Experience with Outdoorsy and PayPal Payment Option.

I rented an RV van through Outdoorsy for a holiday trip and decided to use the 0% interest payment option through PayPal, allowing me to pay off the rental in four installments. To set it up, I linked my credit card to my PayPal account, expecting a smooth transaction. However, I was unexpectedly charged interest on my credit card.

When I contacted Outdoorsy for assistance, I received no support. They simply directed me to PayPal, stating they received the payment from PayPal and that the issue was not theirs to resolve. They also mentioned that other customers had experienced the same issue with this payment offer. If that's the case, why is Outdoorsy still offering a payment option with these known issues?

While I understand that PayPal may be at fault, Outdoorsy is the one offering this payment option on their platform. As such, they should have taken more responsibility in resolving the problem. Instead, they passed me off to PayPal, leaving me frustrated and without any resolution.

Additionally, Outdoorsy should have made it clear on their platform that customers choosing this payment option must first be approved by PayPal. When I selected the payment plan, the process appeared seamless. But when I called PayPal, they asked if I had received an approval email. I had received an email authorizing the payment to Outdoorsy, Inc., but it turns out this was not the approval email they meant.

It’s incredibly disappointing that Outdoorsy, a company that provides services to customers, showed no interest in addressing my concerns once they had received their payment. The lack of customer service and accountability is unacceptable. I will not be using Outdoorsy again and strongly advise others to be cautious when selecting payment options on their platform.

reviewTitle: Better hope your van doesn't get…
reviewDescription:
Better hope your van doesn't get damaged 

I am a host on Outdoorsy, and am supper disappointed with their insurance team. 

I rented my van out to someone who put a dent in the back door. The van has been in the shop for 2 months, and I am still trying to sort things out with the insurance. 

When a renter rents your van, they have to purchase insurance on the vehicle. That way if something happens, you as a host are 100% covered. IDEALLY.

The initial amount the insurance paid out to me was way too low to cover the cost of repairs. So I kept asking the shop to send supplements for the repair. 2 months of back and forth and slow communication later, I am almost out of the woods, only to find out that the insurance might not cover the whole job!! So, I am potentially looking at paying out of pocket to fix my van that a renter damaged! 

What's wild is they say that it would have been cheaper to replace the door (which I am not sure is true, still looking into it), but the van is converted. If I just slap on a new OEM door, what about all the work that I did - insulation, panelling, finished cedar. For a company that rents out camper vans, you would think this wouldn't even have been a consideration. 

All this to say, if you are a van owner, I would not recommend that you rent your van out through this company based on my experience. I certainly will not be after this. 

I just want my van back--fixed and the way it was before I ever got into this disaster.

I had a HORRIBLE experience with Outdoorsy.  I am writing this 6 months after the experience because every time I thought of them my blood boiled too much to sit and write this. It was such an incredibly frustrating experience. DON'T RENT WITH THIS COMPANY! Choosing them and the specific host was my biggest regret of 2024.  As others have written, their contract denies them of essentially any responsibility and accountability to the renter (and sounds like to the host too), so they just wasted days of our time with vague and unhelpful emails. With their call center clearly in the Philippines, they had polite staff responding to all calls and emails, BUT THEY WERE NOT KNOWLEDGEABLE. Their incompetence wasted a lot of time and added so much stress. They even gave wrong information, providing claims forms to fill out that were nothing to do with what they were asking us to do (albeit giving a deadline to submit them), and wrote an infuriating number of 'apology emails' such as you see following their bad reviews on Trustpilot.  A lot of communications, doing nothing, even when you reach more senior staff. There was also no reasonable protection from their side about the host who was making unreasonable claims to us for $8,900! Owners are required to do a technical checkup of their vehicle every 90 days, and since the damage of the camper on the first day of our rental was mechanical, and then the mechanic who helped us after 2 tires blew out (separate incident) told us the tires should have been changed long before our rental period started, I asked if Outdoorsy had even checked the technical report to ensure that the host had even provided us with a decent vehicle; they admitted they had not and would not check the report. What is the point of requiring this if it's not even part of their process during conflicts between host and renter, at a minimum? I have a lot more to say, but I'm done with Outdoorsy forever. 
[ @ Outdoorsy, as I have said several times this year, please don't send me any more empty apology messages.]

Since then, I've rented from Escape Campervans and the experience was night-and-day; you rent directly from them so you don't risk drama from an individual owner trying to take advantage of you, nor the disclaimers from an intermediary who just takes your money and barely lifts a finger to assist their clients. Their options of vehicles are not as varied obviously, but choosing Outdoorsy and their host just to have a camper with a toilet cost us ENORMOUSLY.

reviewDescription: I rented an RV through “Outdoorsy” in BC Canada from the 9/2 to 9/19.  The Owner’s  name is Natalie and the Host’s is Jason Bashnick.  The entire experience was terrible.   The RV was ok but in a fairly poor state given it’s only 5 years old.  There were screws popping out of the bodywork here and there, a chipped windscreen, wardrobe door hanging off and every draw was full of the owners clutter leaving nowhere really to put our stuff.  Nonetheless we took the RV and had a great first part of our holiday. Then the nightmare began.   The breaks on the RV failed on Sunday 9/15 at 1pm.   We managed to pull into a gas station and called Outsoorsy for help…..eventually they told us help was on the way….then they called back to say it wasn’t but if we wanted to pay for a mechanic to come out we could? hello???.   They suggest calling the host who basically said not his problem call Outsoorsy……to be fair to Natalie she texted a few times to tell me to try this and try that but nothing corrected the fault.   Outdoorsy continued to be pretty useless by constantly telling us we had help on the way.  Eventually a breakdown truck arrived 27 hours later after we spend the night at the gas station.   The RV was towed back to Vancouver and we had to check into a hotel for the remainder of our trip.   Since then we have been trying,  unsuccessfully, to claim for the loss of the last four days of our vacation and our unintended hotel expenses.   Outdoorsy have been frankly utterly useless.  I would never recommend anyone use this company to book anything.   The owner basically blamed us for destroying the brakes in her vehicle…..I drive heavy goods vehicles regularly so I know that this is not the case.   Even if that were the case they should have insurance to cover that and the renters loss…….but seemingly they don’t want to even discuss a settlement……and as I said OUTDOORSY are useless DO NOT USE THEM. I am not going to have to use lawyers to fight this as I am not going to be rolled over as a matter of Principle.

reviewTitle: I have been a long time host and renter, with Outdoorsy and others
reviewDescription:
I have been a long time renter and host, for 8yrs with Outdoorsy, as I recall. I'm my experience, they have a fantastic website and process. At days end, they are middle men. I felt that they handled CV19 when business was ten fold, smoothly when they were likely under staffed and many inexperienced renters, we're on the road renting RVs because airplanes were shutdown. I host in CO, a tourist state and if I had 20 Airstreams, I could have rented them all, easily. I can only imagine how hard Outdoorsy and other RV vendors worked to try to keep millions of units and millions of personalities Happy! It's a law of averages, and you'll always have some negatives, but as you can see it's 5% and when I was a kid, 95% was an A+.  Having rented and hosted for years I've learned any negative review is just like anything, it's based on personality and expectations, unless there is a blatant neglect of communication. Otherwise, personality plays a role, and I'm just as guilty of having too high expectations and no different than a dating website, every profile has a certain effort to market, whether price point, or allowing Pets when other do not.
So, I say, always look at the big picture and the % of positive stars as some people post negatively based on emotion, or one-sided points of views, for example, host or renter. I recommend you consider the RVs age first, as age has wear/tear, and match that age to the competitive cost. Photos are great on sewing the design and general unit, but it doesn't show parts and like an aged car, truck, computer, what have you, age plays a role. Personally, I am thankful for Outdoors, introducing me to some fantastic, renters and I truly enjoy RVs and love being a Host, seeing renters come home after their journey and family vacation, all relaxed and happy. You can see the stress that has left their shoulders from PickUp to DropOff. BUT that said, there will always be certain personalities that you just can't live up to their expectations, and as a Host, you just tell them, text me, call me, let me know how I can help. We will handle any issues that comes up, if any, work with RV repair vendors, pay them, and upon drop off, if determined it wasn't Wear/Tear, we have deposits to reimburse us, but we just want you to enjoy, and so does Outdoors. It's a vacation, time to relax and enjoy and avoid making Mountains out of MoleHills as they say, as it's all comes out in the wash.  And over many years, I have issues as both renter and host, always minor but even issues with Brand New trucks or Rvs, it can happen, so keep comms open, that's what I recommend. No one wants a bad vacation for anyone, and that certainly not why Outdoorsy and Hosts option this industry. I saw this as I've had 1 or 2 personalities who tend, finding to complain whether RV, other industries / business they get involved in, they just tend to demand and it's just characteristic, no matter who the vendor may be. In today's world, it's hard to connect with this persona as they communicate one-way, and talk at you, instead of with you. I've seen very very few, but it's exaggeration and characteristic of the submitter, but to submit 1 star, even start to a middleman vendor, seems more emotional, than offering readers the positives. Why blame Outdoorsy unless the entire vacation was unruly managed start to finish, canceled or destroyed, at their direct blame. I just think emotions are not good reviews. With a great of Tv experience, I know some folks get upset about small things where others accept that "life happens". Wear and tear exists when you rent units over 5yrs, it's part of Rv life, there are tons of forums you should look into, per model you wish to rent, as that can help too.  Life is short, enjoy your vacation and just know that the older the "rental unit" it's more likely to have some small glitch occur, and don't try to fix or work on a Hosts Rv, without working with them, as I've seen people break things trying to Google How To, so let your Host handle it. And if an RV repairman need to get involved, hand it off to Host, they need to know who's working on their unit, and they know their Rv best, or either way, let them pay direct, hand it off to them, so you can go back to enjoying your time with family, friends or your journey.
Two way communication is the answer. As long as there is two-way communication, there is no issue that can't be resolved. And no need to blame Outdoorsy or other vendors in general, your our Guest(s). Just work together and you will be in the 95%, I say, from my years in Rv works. Hope this helps!

However, the Toilet/Tank was full and clogged first off.  I had to pay for the state park to let me empty it. Last, one of my slide outs were stuck in and and wouldn't go back out.  Mind you I emailed outdoorsy the photos plus receipts and I had to hire a mobile RV Mechanic to make the slide out work again.  They did a review and said they can't use the customers deposit for the damages.  (EXCUSE ME!) I understood that's what a deposit is for.  So that if something breaks that the deposit will help pay for the damages.. HUH, I guess not.  Either way Ive emailed back and forth with the piece of crap company and gotten nowhere.  They refuse to take the money out of the customers deposit and not pay me back for the mobile mechanic or the waste fee.  To me it's just the point of it all.  I do not recommend anyone using outdoorsy for your RV rental hosting needs.  BEWARE!!!

reviewTitle: At a loss for our holidays because customer service did nothing to help us
reviewDescription:
After booking an RV we tried contacting the host to set up arrangements.  The host wasn't responding. I emailed host once 3 weeks and a second time one week before reaching out to customer support for help.  

Customer supports response was that they give hosts 24-48 hours to respond because "we understand life can throw your routine off".  I replied with our timeline of trying to contact the host and asked that they help us reach the host. I haven't heard anything back from customer support after this. 

I emailed multiple times and I called multiple times. No one would answer the call, and once I was on hold for 30 minutes (no exaggeration) to only be disconnected after a recording told me no one was available to take my call. 

I reached out on social media with my situation and got a response to call or email customer support for assistance... really?!  That response just added more to my frustration.

I had to cancel my reservation one week before our holidays because of the unresponsive host and lack of support from the folks at outdoorsy.  Thankfully the amount was deposited back into my account.  

Now aside from this frustrating experience with outdoorsy and their host, I am $80 out of pocket for the "service fee" that wasn't refunded... Really?!  What service did I pay for??  A whole lot of wasted time on my end for trying to get service, I should charge outdoorsy for my time, not the other way around. 

But the worst part of all this is my family and I have been looking forward to this, our annual holiday that we work very hard to afford and now we don't know what we're going to do.  We have a campground  booked with nothing to bring with us. So to quote your customer service I guess "life can throw your routine off" but replace "life" with "outdoorsy".

From this experience, my take away is outdoorsy doesn't care. At no point did anyone on your end try to resolve this matter.  You have profited off my service fee and my family is now at a loss on what we will do.  An appropriate action on your part would have been to 1) Respond to our concerns first and foremost 2) Try to resolve our concerns by helping us contact the host 3) If the host remained unresponsive, find another solution if possible.  Would there have been another rental you could have helped us manage? We even would have paid more for a bigger camper if necessary.  But unfortunately none of that happened.

No one wins from this experience! Outdoorsy, sure you made money from my service fee, but you had to refund $800 and our holidays are one week away and we don't know what we're going to do.

Outdoorsy if you do care about the people you are providing services to and making a profit off of, you still have time to make this right for me and my family. I have little hope, but at this time I have little options.

reviewDescription:
A FUIIIIIIIIIIIIRRRRRRRR !!!! et c'est peu dire ! Voleurs, ARNAQUE  et MENSONGES 

Mes amis et moi avons réservé un camping-car pour 10 jours afin de visiter le parc des Rocheuses.
Nous avons réservé quasiment 1 an à l'avance.  Nous sommes tombés en panne après une quarantaine de km sur l’autoroute suite à un pneus qui a éclaté. Le propriétaire nous a répondu de voir directement avec la plateforme car nous avions payé 500 CAD pour une assistance tout risque. COMMENT DIRE..... 
 Nous sommes restés bloqués sur l’autoroute (donc dans un environnement très peu commode et sécuritaire) sans eau ni électricité pendant 21h en essayant en vain de contacter Outdoorsy pour être dépannés. Nous n’avons à aucun moment été transporté en sécurité pour passer une nuit convenable... Ni même rassuré. 
Nous avons passé pas moins de 6h au téléphone, enchainant les numéros, les boîtes vocales toute la nuit avec eux, afin d’obtenir gain de cause et enfin être dépannée. Il fallait répéter en vain la même chose... étions mis en attente des heures durant.

Je  suis vraiment INSATISFAITE, voir HORRIPILÉE par si peu de savoir vivre,  d’avoir été aussi peu assistée dans cette situation anodine pourtant alors que nous avions payé une assurance complète et toute option, à quoi bon ? 

De plus, après des heures , ET  sous les conseils de notre correspondant technique, nous avons rendu le camping-car car il était en trop mauvais état (fenêtre de toit cassé, pare-brise fissuré, générateur non fonctionnel, portes de placards cassées…). 

Ce dernier nous a assuré que nous serions remboursés car un mauvais état de véhicule est contraire à leur politique... que des paroles et des mensonges.... 
 Résultat ?? après plus d’un mois de négociations nous n’avons pas été remboursé intégralement, (même pas la moitié de l'assurance tout risque, et aucun dédommagement du camping car)  et Outdoorsy se dédouane de toute responsabilité… nous avons pourtant toutes les preuves des mails envoyés et des conversations téléphoniques 
prétextant qu'ils ne peuvent pas faire plus que ça...

Je ne recommande pas du tout cette plateforme en ligne très peu fiable, mensongère, c'est une belle arnaque.

reviewDescription:
Giant red flags. We rented a trailer that had a completely dead battery, and we were given no idea by the owner. 

We provided Outdoorsy time stamped photos of the battery going dead within 45 minutes, even after hours of running a generator to charge it. We lost all our food overnight, we had no heat. After weeks of internal review outdoorsy sent us a photo of their terms of service with yellow highlighting on the line saying "failing to use the vehicle in compliance with all instructions and warnings provided by us and hosts." Not sure how we could have better used a dead battery that was not disclosed. We did buy additional propane as the owner did disclose that he didn't even check if they were filled. Hideous experience and clearly Outdoorsy is going to back their owners over the customers. Buyer beware.

reviewDescription:
Since Outdoorsy refuses to post my negative review on their site I will post it here. 

Backstory - 

Outdoorsy provides a 14 day window to provide a review. I was about to post my review on their site at 11pm on the 14th day and it said I was no longer able to post as they operate in central time and it was already 1am the next day so I missed their window by 1 hour. 

I was then asked if the review was positive as they could manually post a positive review for me but not a negative one. 

Can someone please explain how this is a fair policy that they can manually post a positive review but not a negative one? It’s screams scam all over and fake positive reviews. 

Here is my review of my RV rental experience: 

RENTER BEWARE
As first time RV renters, we were extremely disappointed with our stay and the treatment of our group. We had to depart a day earlier than expected and had called the host to let them know. I asked what we needed to do before leaving and he said just make sure to turn everything off and close the door. They never mentioned anything about cleaning the RV before we left, nor was it mentioned to us when we checked-in. Later that evening, we received a message saying we had left the RV in a ‘state’ and that there were stains on the cushions and blinds and they were charging us $775 for excess cleaning and to replace the items that were supposedly stained. 

We were honestly shocked by this message and offered our apologies and even to come back and clean the RV due to the misunderstanding of what the prep fee covers as we were under the impression it covered the cleaning (it literally states on Outdoorsy that the fee is to cover professional cleaning and required maintenance checks). 

We were told that they were unavailable and had already cleaned half the RV. We explained that we were under the impression the prep fee covered the cleaning but then the host began to scold me telling me they had left cleaning supplies and that I was making excuses as to why it wasn’t cleaned. 

They did not care at all that it was a misunderstanding and that if we had known we were supposed to clean it, we would have done so.

We feel taken advantage of as first time RV renters as there is no way it would cost them $200 of time to clean the RV, $431.15 to replace the dinette cushion with a tiny yogurt drink stain or $144.45 to replace the blinds that we had supposedly stained. 

Hopefully this review can help the next person renting so they do not get scammed like we did. Make sure you read the paperwork before you sign it and do not be too trusting of hosts. Also make sure to take photos and document before and after as we forgot to take photos before we left and therefore had no evidence of our own to dispute their claim.

I've looked into this further on my end and reached out to you personally to discuss it in greater detail. I look forward to connecting with you.
 
Jaime
Outdoorsy Support

reviewTitle: I’ve hosted my RV for 2-years on the…
reviewDescription:
I’ve hosted my RV for 2-years on the Outdoorsy Platform and finally removed myself completely this week. The service/support, App Issues and overall inadequate screening processes are not worth the effort.

To use one example - Outdoorsy “verifies” drivers and renters through a “proprietary process” which it states gives you the piece of mind that a driver is legally able to drive a motor vehicle. One renter ended up causing damage while driving my RV and at the end of the trip the Outdoorsy Team went to file a claim and found he hadn’t had auto insurance in more than 2-years and his driver’s license had been revoked by the state. The claim/repair took several more weeks to be approved and has still yet to be resolved as they worked through how to appropriately handle the situation. I wouldn’t trust someone who shows up to take your vehicle from the App; there are great, honest renters out there but Outdoorsy’s policies don’t allow you to differentiate.

*9/11/24 Update: I’ve been contacted internally by the Outdoorsy Team but can share that there still hasn’t been any progress on my Claim Status. The uninsured driver who damaged my vehicle submitted fake Insurance Information and Outdoorsy is “waiting to hear from them” before they proceed. I’ve left a handful of messages with Progressive to no avail. Be ready for a multi-month repair if you have any damage done to your RV.
reviewCompanyResponse:

reviewTitle: JUST STAY AWAY FROM OUTDOORSY-READ THE REVIEWS AND THE FINE PRINT
reviewDescription:
JUST STAY AWAY FROM OUTDOORSY! 
Today is 7/29/24 and I am still dealing with a bogus scam claim from 7/4/24, 25 days later!  I have spent several hours over many many days trying to communicate with Outdoorsy and I deeply regret using them. 

We are experienced campers.  We decided to rent an RV this time to make things easy (ha!).  We paid about $400 (in addition to rental, insurance, and Outdoorsy fees) to have it delivered and set up. During our rental over the 4th of July, the power cord + surge protector failed...resulting in a smoking, melted plug.  We were left with no power, no A/C, and we are in Texas in the middle of Summer. To our surprise, the the owner filed a damage claim against us which resulted in many headaches and countless hours of wasted time-with no real help from Outdoorsy. In fact, whenever I called, I could only speak with someone in the PHILIPPINES-never the same person, and never a last name.  Sometimes someone would respond via email, but would not be available by phone.  There was always a promise to escalate, but no follow-through.

We had actually purchased the stationary insurance (also called excess insurance), which we learned that they defer to YOUR PERSONAL AUTO POLICY FIRST.  When I went back, I eventually found this in the fine print, but had to click through several pages and links to get to-essentially a rabbit hole. After the owner was told by our insurance company that this was not a qualified claim (and subsequently denied), he then pursued it with the Outdoorsy stationary policy-which was also denied. He told our insurance company that he would pursue it through the security deposit. They then emailed to ask us to approve a charge of $400-to pay for their faulty equipment-even after 2 denials stating that we were not responsible for their equipment failing. We declined. We cannot get anyone at Outdoorsy to help us with this, though we have reached out via phone and email multiple times. Today we learned that Outdoorsy has charged our card for ANOTHER $750-the original amount of our deposit.

After another call and another request to escalate-they are telling us that they have released it.  I have yet to confirm that with my CC company, but will need to make sure that all future attempts to charge us will be denied.  

A side note of the stationary/excess policy-they also add an additional charge for roadside assistance-which you should not need if you are not towing the vehicle.  In fact, the policy becomes null and void if you move the camper for any reason on a stationary policy.  

We would never even think of trusting Outdoorsy again-it's really just a platform, taking a cut, and cheaply outsourcing their customer service. 

There is no protection and no one to call who can do anything to help.

reviewTitle: I've rented my vehicles via Outdoorsy…
reviewDescription:
I've rented my vehicles via Outdoorsy for three years now.  On a recent rental, one of my clients had a minor issue with their rental.  We discussed and decided a small refund would be best suited to resolve the situation.  Outdoorsy instructs you to call them if you would like to refund your client for something other than a cancellation.  When I called I was told that Outdoorsy would keep their 25% cut of the refund.  That's right, Outdoorsy keeps their 25% "host service fee" on the amount of money you REFUND as the host. 

Outdoorsy is not much of a service but provides a great disservice to both the renter, to whom they charge exorbitant fees and force the sale of often redundant insurance products but also the owner, from whom they take a 25% cut, even when the owner didn't ultimately keep those funds.  

I would love to see another business disrupt Outdoorsy and provide a quality, peer to peer, platform for rentals.  Most renters already have adequate auto insurance that covers their rental. Most owners need little more than a platform/market on which to rent their rigs and a payment processing service that allows for security deposits to be held appropriately.   What nobody needs is Outdoorsy in the middle of it all, bilking all parties for every penny they can. 

Follow up to Outdoorsy's response- yes, that's correct, you are keeping 25% of money the owner does not keep.  There is no confusion here nor is anyone implying that an additional fee is charged.

Folks, this is why Outdoorsy releases 100% of your payout after handoff; it ensures they are keeping 25% of that money no matter what happens during or after the rental.  No other platform keeps their fee, particular a 25% one, on money you don't actually net.  In this case I wanted to refund my renter $750.  Trouble is, Outdoorsy only paid me $562.50.  I can't refund money I never received and Outdoorsy refuses to return their $187.50 cut of those funds that, again, are not in my bank account, to the renter.  They refuse to explain what they are keeping that $187.50 for and I don't know a soul who could rationalize why that money wouldn't be refunded to the renter.  

I can't post images here but would love to share a breakdown of a trip with the public to shed light on the exorbitant fees Outdoorsy hits both renter and owner with.  It's classic gig economy corporate shenanigans where a real service/platform is provided but everyone gets ripped off in the process of utilizing it.

The fact that they want a 25% cut of money you don't get paid really says it all though.
reviewCompanyResponse:
Hi Wanderlust AV,

Please allow me to clarify how fees work in relation to refunds. Outdoorsy assesses a service fee on the booking total for both the host and the guest. These fees are nonrefundable, even if a refund is granted by the host. However, there's no additional fee assessed on refunded amounts. I'll be reaching out to you personally to provide some additional insight and address any other concerns you may have. I look forward to connecting with you. 
 
Jaime

I (single female with toddler and two dogs) rented an RV through Outdoorsy and it TURNED OFF on a two lane highway where I had zero cell reception. Luckily, there was a view area to the right and I rolled it in. When a couple pulled in with cell service, the Outdoorsy support rep told me to LEAVE MY DOGS in the RV and get to safety with my child. When I asked her if it would be lifted on a tow truck (wondering how my dogs would handle sliding to the back when it was lifted), she told me yes and still insisted I leave them. The tow truck arrived well after dark and didn't contact me until close to 11pm. Who would have checked on my dogs during those 9 or so hours??? I am still so furious. Thankfully kind strangers gave all of us a ride. We ended up spending 8k on rental cars, hotels and other incidentals while we waited for the RV to get fixed so we could drive it home. After several days, we left without it and left it up to the owners to get it (it's still at a mechanic 7 hours away with most of our stuff). When I told customer support what had happened, they insisted I get some sort of restitution from the owners and did NOTHING for me. W t f did I even buy insurance for if they weren't going to cover anything? They are rude, demeaning, and only out to make money for themselves. They did not care AT ALL that I was stuck on a freeway with a toddler and 2 dogs- they didn't even send a tow for HOURS later! DO NOT USE OUTDOORSY. You will regret it. Trust me.

Reply to below reply from Outdoorsy….
It’s unconscionable that Outdoorsy is trying to defend their ridiculous dashboard system. I set up a discount code of 10% for Repeat Renters and there is a button where you can turn on the discount code. Of course I think that that means I’m turning on the code for WHEN I GIVE THE CODE TO A GUEST but NO,  instead what happened is their system gave the discount to every single renter and six renters later, I caught it! Meaning I never gave the discount code to the other customers but yet they got a 10% discount on their rental!! And then Outdoorsy tells me it’s my fault because I turned the button on. How is it even logical that I would think that every renter would get a 10% discount if I never gave them the discount code to enter in? It’s so stupid I can’t even imagine how they’re trying to justify this!! Of course the guests never said anything and Outdoorsy blames it on me.
 $409 in lost revenue and they only refunded me $100 after emailing me back-and-forth with robotic replies, I kept asking them for a phone call and it took three emails to finally get someone to call me that was unhelpful and they gave me back $100.  You still owe me $309!

It appears you have removed the code from your account now which will remove the discount trigger, but please don't hesitate to reach out to our support team if you need any additional assistance. Dale R.

reviewDescription: Outdoorsy is a purposefully difficult service. Luckily I was able to talk w the owner directly for most things. We drove for 14 hours in a terrible storm and found a small crack in the windshield upon returning home. Thought, no big deal, the insurance paperwork I read said they cover windshield cracks. Owner was very understanding. Since I paid $359 for MANDATORY insurance and then $500 extra for a security deposit, the owner and I figured getting the crack fixed would be a breeze. I have not been able to speak to anyone from the US to help me. I am a firefighter and my husband is a retired NAVY SEAL. We want to do the right thing for the owner and also have this company honor the insurance. They have directed me to a long contract that was “signed” but NOT by us. They’ve also changed the terms of the insurance agreement multiple times. This is fraud. They take good peoples money and then will not do the ethical thing by fixing the crack for the owner. After paying $850 in addition to the rent and fees, this more than covers the owner being able to get his RV repaired and for me to not pay any more money out of pocket. I paid $1500 to rent. $850 for insurance and security deposit. The owner only received $829 for the week rental. This is not morally or ethically acceptable. They do not want to serve their customers, the owners or respect that hard working Americans and veterans do not deserve to be manipulated and abused.

reviewTitle: I rented a travel trailer that stated…
reviewDescription:
I rented a travel trailer that stated was "brand spanking new" that had too much to list here wrong with it. Broken awning, bathroom vent, etc. Through observation, video, and photos this was not new or even close. But what really turned things was me being alerted by someone at an intersection in the midst of returning vehicle that one of the tires was making a "bad sound". My wife and I rolled down windows and could definitely hear what she was saying. Tried to see if I could determine the cause. Thought it might be one tire that looked like missing something. At that time, I messaged owner rep to say something was wrong and asked if I should stop or continue to return. Was told camper had always had an issue with a tire and that I should be fine. A few miles later the tread on the tire separated causing a massive blowout and damage to the undercarriage. Was told in this process that there was no visible evidence of outside damage and tire appeared to be faulty. Also, through this process was told that tires were new by owner (worded in such a fashion as if to release her of any liability because this doesn't happen to new tires, while failing to recognize this is where they happen the most). Was also told that there had been a blowout on the same tire 2 months prior. Was told that is why "I" have insurance and within hours my $500 deductible was taken. I disputed the deductible because I am to be given a safe and reliable vehicle to rent. Was not provided a safe and reliable vehicle, and then when something on the camper failed, the expectation was of me to fix it or pay for it. This would be the final response from my dispute...

Hi Bradley,
 
After an investigation and careful consideration of the evidence provided by both parties, we have decided in your favor.
 
We believe you should be refunded $250 from the security deposit claimed. While we uncovered that the tires were over 5 years old already, we believe you should shoulder $250 since the damage was incurred during your rental. 

And here is my response and reason for 1 star....

    Think I will consult with someone on this. Aside from a contradiction within your response which has to leave someone lying by stating they were outdated tires when I had multiple people state tires were new (with one very important individual stating they were new and faulty), it is the holding the renter accountable due to the actions of an owner that finds this resolution appalling to me.

       If what you state in your resolution is correct, then the lack of action by an owner to provide safe and reliable tires has taken place. If what the owner and her handyman stated (and documented) is correct in stating the tires were newly purchased, it was they who purchased and installed an inferior product and not myself. Had I punctured a tire, went to purchase a new tire on my own, the tire then exploded causing damage, I would most certainly be singing a different tune. But your resolution seems to state that I must pay 250 dollars because someone provided me with an unsafe and unreliable vehicle for rent. It's hard for me to not think of a thousand scenarios at play here that would represent the same type of conclusion. Such as, what if an owner has an engine failing?  Was repaired with substandard or improper parts that result in complete engine failure while on the next rental. Would the responsibility of the renter be to replace the engine? If so, then there are no protections from the owner's doing whatever they want, with WHATEVER they want, because of no consequence. And I have a significant issue with this because the initial response from the owner of my rental was just that. And I quote, "that's why you have insurance".

      As far as I'm concerned, the first and foremost priority from the owner is to provide a safe and reliable vehicle for rental. The fact this did not happen should instantly render any contract null and void and prohibited anyone from obtaining my deposit. I seem to believe most people would agree with me. And what adds insult to injury is that you have a host who is falsely advertising a vehicle that is not as it is portrayed.

     What also bothers me greatly is the fact that I reached out to the owner's representative prior to any damage occurring to state there was an issue. Specifically asked him whether to continue or not. Only to be told they have always had an issue with a tire and I should continue.

     I will say basically the same thing I said to the owner. This is absolutely about principle. I do not believe I should pay 500/250/or even 5 dollars. The responsibility of repairs needed due to faulty parts and equipment provided by an owner should not rest on the renter in any shape or form but that of the owner.
reviewCompanyResponse:
Hi Brad, 
 
I'm so sorry to hear you had a poor experience on your trip due to a tire blowout and you're dissatisfied with the dispute resolution provided by our Resolutions team. Your time is valuable, so thank you for taking a moment to share your feedback and bring this to our attention. I'll be investigating further on my end, and I'll reach out to you personally. 
 
Jaime
Outdoorsy Support

reviewDescription: great platform, but website not user friendly

I hired a RV through Outdoorsy through August 2023 (20 nights). They sub-let to another company in the days/week before commencement - being told the campervan I booked (5 months earlier) was no longer available. A 'similar' vehicle was available when I arrived (same price, same conditions).  Wrong.
No stealth camping as few curtains (!), no pillows (!), limited stove or utensils (!), no outdoor seating or  cover (!).  Not what I expected or paid for ....

But worse was the developing problems with the two-year old automatic vehicle (a Mercedes van!).
From Day 1 it had problems - wrong gear, shuddering forward, over-revving - which was increasingly dangerous when driving on US freeways at half the set speed.  Stop-start - stop - shudder - stop - start - stop altogether - so multiple calls to the intermediary company, then Outdoorsy.

Outdoorsy provides intermittent phone calls/call-backs during the afternoon, leading to a request to drive (!) to the nearest specialist repair workshop (30 miles away).  Tried on back-roads .. but same problems and the vehicle again died half way, leaving me at the edge of the road in 108F degree heat in the middle of no-where ... for 15 hours /overnight!   
  
Many Outdoorsy phone calls - no action - before next morning the tow vehicle was sent to the previous (wrong) address!

Eventually, towed away to the repair shop.  Long wait before they diagnosed the problem.  Meantime, Outdoorsy told me I was one my own - they wouldn't supply an alternative vehicle.  I was expected to start all over again with a new hire (whilst marooned in the middle of nowhere!).  
"Not good enough, we have a paid contract" I said - and let me speak to the manager.  It took a week before a manager sent an email back, asking about my problem !!!!   

And fortunately for me, the dealer found and fixed the 'drive' cables/electronics that had been chewed through by 'varmints' prior to my hire.  So after two delayed days of hassle I was able to continue.

LESSONS:  
Outdoorsy would not meet their contractual conditions when it got hard....they made their vehicle replacement problems mine ...
Outdoorsy is better at PR and marketing than delivering ...   
Outdoorsy subsequently made it very difficult to get a refund for the lost two days of travel ... 
Don't believe their promotional spin .. my experience says you're on you're own if there's a vehicle replacement problem.

reviewDescription:
The disclosed fees were already quite high, but to then make a separate charge for an undisclosed, 3% foreign transaction fee, felt wrong and odd.
I’d deliberately used a debit card which doesn’t charge foreign transaction fees for the US trip. Nowhere else did I encounter a foreign/international transaction fee.

Update: Your reply seems at odds with the Chase UK terms, which are as follows.

“ You can use your Chase card to pay for things or withdraw cash outside the UK (cash withdrawal limits apply).

We don’t charge fees when you use your card abroad and we'll apply the current Mastercard exchange rate to any purchases you make in the local currency. Local banks may charge their own fees when you use one of their cash machines and you should make sure you understand what these are before making a withdrawal. You can use your numberless card to make free withdrawals at any Chase Bank ATM in the United States.”
As I said, I’ve not experienced these fees before when using this card with other vendors. Are you sure it’s not your card processor fees, which you’re passing on?
reviewCompanyResponse:
Hi David,

Thank you for posting a review, and I'm sorry to hear you're dissatisfied with the foreign transaction fee. I'd like to reassure you that this fee is not an Outdoorsy fee or charge but a fee stipulated by your card payment processor when using a card issued outside of the country in which you’re renting. Transparency is very important to us, so please allow me to clarify that when processing a reservation charge on our platform, a warning of the foreign transaction fee is displayed before checkout. If you have any questions or concerns, please feel free to contact our 24/7 Customer Support team via chat or by calling 877-723-7232.

reviewTitle: We love the peace of mind we get…
reviewDescription: We love the peace of mind we get renting our van through Outdoorsy. Knowing my guests and I are 100% covered if anything were to happen makes the process so much more enjoyable. The app and platform for hosting needs improvement but I think Outdoorsy is working on it.

The company does a good job of providing a platform to connector renters with rv/trailer owners. The fees are reasonable in comparison to other such sites and the experience is generally more easy going. If I could provide a piece of constructive criticism, as an owner I regularly receive booking requests or messages while I am away from my PC, but the phone app (at least the iPhone app) does not have a "host" mode that allows me to review the booking request and it can be tricky to get the information pulled up on my mobile browser sometimes. 
Additionally, I have found that I cannot rely on the message-to-text capability. I have regularly received inquiries or messages from guests that do not show up as a text message on my phone while others do. It would provide a much better experience if this were more consistent.
Communication
Wouldn’t change a thing. Everything is straight forward and anytime I’ve needed help customer service has been great
Outdoorsy has been great, it’s super easy and convenient. There has been no issues

Beware of Outdoorsy's Misleading Cancellation Policy and Poor Customer Service
I recently had an incredibly disappointing experience with Outdoorsy, a company that seemed promising for RV rentals. My ordeal began when I booked a van for a trip in June, well in advance. Realizing the costs were too steep for my budget 10 days later, I decided to cancel, expecting a reasonable refund considering the trip was nearly 5 months (June 2024) away. To my shock, I discovered that Outdoorsy had a hidden trap in their cancellation policy, costing me over $1000 in cancellation fees, for a service I never utilized.
The van owner, under the guise of convenience, persuaded me to switch from Turo to Outdoorsy, which I suspect was a maneuver to exploit Outdoorsy's cancellation policy for his financial gain. Outdoorsy's policy, which mercilessly penalizes customers for cancellations made outside a narrow 48-hour window post-booking, is both unfair and predatory, especially when cancellations are made well in advance of the booking date.
Their defense? A rigid adherence to a policy that favors hosts at the expense of customers, trapping hard-earned money with no service provided. The fact that this trip was months away and the rushed booking process facilitated by the van owner makes this policy even more egregious.
Outdoorsy's lack of transparency and customer empathy has left me feeling cheated and disillusioned. Their policies do not protect the interests of the customer but serve to enrich themselves and their hosts at our expense. I caution anyone considering using Outdoorsy for their RV rental needs to think twice. You may find yourself, like me, out of pocket for services you never received, entangled in a web of unfair policies designed to profit from your change of plans.
Final Rating: 1/5 Stars
Avoid Outdoorsy if you value your money and seek a fair, customer-centric rental experience. There are other platforms out there that respect and protect their customers' interests far better.
