reviewTitle: Our family of 6 got scammed by Outdoorsy!!
reviewDescription: Our family of 6 is planning a 10 day road trip this summer and wanted one vehicle comfortable for us all. I found the perfect vehicle on Outdoorsy, absolutely stunning! We were all so excited. The booking was glitchy and instead of charging a deposit with the reservation I was charged the full $1,300 for the trip that doesn't start for 5 months! I contacted customer service, they fixed it and charged me $757 in Feb. for a Jul. trip, balance later. I never received a confirmation from the vehicle owner, just from Outdoorsy. A few weeks later my daughter inquired about vehicle features and found out the vehicle was sold a year and a half ago and the owner can't get Outdoorsy to remove it. I contacted customer service to cancel and refund all my money. I was told they won't refund my money unless the vehicle owner verifies my claim. Now the vehicle owner has become unresponsive too. If you try to cancel the reservation on Outdoorsy they keep a service charge of $145. It's a total mess and Outdoorsy accepts no responsibility in validating a reservation, or vehicle availability or promptly refunding my money. For a finish, I'm now disputing the charge with my credit card company under fraud protection. Outdoorsy took my money, won't refund it, for a vehicle that isn't available. These people are scammers! Watch out!
reviewCompanyResponse:
Hi Laurie, 
 
I'm so sorry to hear your host has sold their van and failed to remove their listing from Outdoorsy, causing you to be able to instant book the reservation. This is most definitely not the intended experience we'd prefer our guests to have. Upon review, I see that you submitted previous feedback and are currently working with a team member to resolve this. Rest assured, we want to support you in resolving this matter as quickly as possible. My teammate Dale is investigating this thoroughly and will be reaching back out to you regarding the next steps. Please bear in mind that if you submit a dispute with your bank, it could take up to 90 days to be resolved, and we won't be able to process a refund on our end when there's an open chargeback. We appreciate your patience and your feedback. Please watch your email for a response from Dale, as we hope to resolve this as soon as possible.
 
Jaime 
Outdoorsy Support

reviewDescription:
Edited to add that following my review outdoorsy have finally sent us on the 'invoices' from the owner which is a screen grab from Amazon and a shopping cart for another website! The owner hasn't even ordered the parts they've charged us for and outdoorsy have still taken the money from us!



Steer clear!! We visited LA and hired an RV through outdoorsy for ten nights to see some sights and enjoy a family holiday. 
On delivery of the RV the owners told us they'd been up all night trying to fix the slide out after the family that rented it and finished with it the night before our trip has broken it and we need to be extra careful with it as it was not fully fixed, handed us a screwdriver and sent us on our way. Should have run a mile then! 
First night we struggled with the slide out and the over cab bunk wouldn't work, asked owners for help and they said they'd work it out and let us know the morning after!! So 3 beds down and no where for the kids to sleep we finally found a you tube video on how to over ride the system and bring the beds down.
Long story short we had several issues with the slide out and the owners advise was to not use it, but since we'd rented the RV as a family of 6 we needed the slide out working in order to have enough space to sleep. Ended up having to stay in one place so that the owners who were exceptionally rude and aggressive over the phone could come meet us to try fix the issue, they managed a quick fix but told us we'd only be able to perform one more slide action and so we couldn't visit anywhere else in it and had to drive in one from San Fran to LA. During one of our drives an awning over one slide out actually came loose and on advice from the owners my husband and sons had to climb up on to the roof of the RV to remove it!! We stayed in touch with outdoorsy via email during the trip so that they were aware of the issues we were having and they continually reassured us that they would help us claim compensation on return to the UK. 
The owners also advised us that the TVs in the RV didn't work without dvds (despite advertising that it had an outdoors TV entertainment system and two indoors) so we never used the TVs.

When we delivered the RV back to the owner she herself didn't turn up and instead sent her husband who rushed us out saying he was late for a school play (they chose the collection time) he wouldn't do a walk round but verbally confirmed happy with the RV and zoomed off within five minutes of us arriving back. 
On return to the UK they've claimed $1300 of our deposit and have only started 'TV and slide out topper' as the reason. We've asked them to confirm what was wrong and what they mean by that but got the response 'tv and slide out topper' 

We've gone through outdoorsy internal review and they took over a month to decide that they don't know who's at fault and cannot provide us with evidence or proof of even a basic description of what they've claimed the money for as they say the money was taken by the host and not outdoorsy (shows as outdoorsy on our statement) 

They do not want to know. I'm not sure how money can be taken without any proof as to what for or why. 

Were assuming the slide out topper means the awning they actually told us to remove and was also missing from the other side of the RV so I'm certain somewhere another family have been charged for that and it's never been fitted or replaced. 

I've warned outdoorsy that this owner needs removing from the platform but as long as it's making them money they don't seem to care. 

Do not book with this company please save yourself the hassle. A month on from our holiday we're still chasing our money back. 

When the initial claim was made on our deposit outdoorsy told us it was just a hold whilst the internal investigation was ongoing and that it wouldn't actually be taken but a few days later it left our account.

Dishonest, unreliable and unhelpful.

reviewDescription: The INSTAmatch feature is fantastic. It notifies me when someone is searching for a rental and matches us up. Outdoorsy is one of the easiest platforms to list my RV for rent.

From the very beginning the people we were dealing with were less than up front about various maintenance issues.  They knew exactly how many miles we were traveling and that we were using the unit to move our 97 year old mother, who had just had a stroke.

Basically there were several things that would fail, cause further damage and even perhaps cause injury that we had to fix.  When we returned the unit the owners claimed there was damage from a slide out unit that, to our knowledge, was working when we dropped the unit off.  We just walked away from the $1500 deposit and then, after dragging things on for a month they want more money.  I billed them for the work we had to perform and hope they just leave it at that.

The impression I got from the process is that leaving things that will cause further damage is a strategy to have an excuse to grab the deposit from unsuspecting renters.  At this point it feels like they are trying to pick us up by our feet to shake all the change out of our pockets. We have already paid thousands of dollars and the maintenance of the vehicle should not be our problem.      

Just beware, I can't speak about all the owners, but these seem to want to take advantage of us.  The extra insurance we purchased has proven unresponsive and the Outdoorsy customer support is offering to try to bring resolution, so will see if this can end any time this year...

Update: The interior damage insurance we purchased did come through, but it took quite a while.  To their credit Outdoorsy did give us a token credit to express good will and acknowledge our inconvenience with the issues we experienced.  Maybe through the process the owners, who are young, will learn about maintaining their equipment.  

Just beware, don't make assumptions and ask questions about the condition of the equipment.  According to the Outdoorsy policy, if the owners disclose a problem and you accept the rental, you become responsible for the problem and any further damage it may cause while in your possession.  If you experience issues with the equipment that require attention, if the owners are not cooperative, you are stuck on the side of the road and may be financially responsible for getting repairs done.

reviewTitle: I’ve left Outdoorsy due to their bad policy
reviewDescription:
I rented out my RV through Outdoorsy. Most of the time things went great. Recently I had a person reserve my RV, and I have a strict cancellation policy so that our rental income is maintained if someone decides they want to cancel. 

Several days before the scheduled pickup, they were deemed “disqualified” for rental by Outdoorsy. 

The renter refused to cancel, and long story short, Outdoorsy says that it’s not the renters fault and not Outdoorsy’s fault. So, I am the one that loses out. 

Outdoorsy should require people to qualify BEFORE they are allowed to rent. However, they don’t because it would put a roadblock to people renting, and that would not benefit them. 

So, because they choose greed over relationships, I have left their platform. I have also convinced 7 people to do the same as Outdoorsy is only interested in protecting themselves and not the people that invest in RVs that even make their platform possible. 

I will make more money off platform, and will be protected by other insurance that allows rentals. Plus, I don’t have to worry about policies that only protect them. 

I gave them a chance to make this right, and they refused. They cost themselves tens of thousands of dollars by not holding my renter to their commitment.
reviewCompanyResponse:

Thanks for sharing your feedback, and I'm sincerely sorry that your experience with our service didn't match your expectations. I'll investigate to see what happened here and will get in touch with you soon to address your concerns.

Dale R.

reviewCompanyResponse:
Hi there, 

I'm so sorry to hear you're dissatisfied with our protection packages. Thank you for taking a moment to share your feedback with us. Please allow me to clarify the insurance a bit more. We partner with Assurant Mutual, and all protection packages will vary depending on the Class, year, make, and model of the RV, and those rates are set at industry standards based on the Actual Cash Value of the RV provided to us by the host. This coverage is required for RVs that are being delivered as well. Similar to an auto rental, RVs must have this episodic coverage to protect both parties should any vandalism, theft, acts of nature, etc. occur while the RV is in your possession.

For more information about our coverages, please visit https://www.outdoorsy.com/help/owners-protection-packages.

Jaime
Outdoorsy Support

reviewTitle: JUST MAKE SURE YOU DON'T HAVE TO FILE A…
reviewDescription:
JUST MAKE SURE YOU DON'T HAVE TO FILE A CLAIM!
JUST MAKE SURE YOU LIST ALL VERIFIED DRIVERS!
Our hosts did not fulfill their commitment to check that my husband and I were listed with Outdoorsy as verified drivers (my husband was not, and he did ALL of the driving). The Outdoorsy website is clunky to use and adding a verified driver is opaque, so I missed this when I made the reservation online. A stone hit the windshield of the Sprinter we rented and the windshield had to be replaced by the owner when we got back. I am not sure why the hosts are complaining on these reviews that they had to file a claim, because our host made us as the renters file the claim. Outdoorsy/Roamly immediately denied it because my husband was not listed as a verified driver (again, the hosts should have checked this before the keys were handed to my husband, a "non-verified" driver). The toll free number for Outdoorsy has customer service people who are trying to help, but it was IMPOSSIBLE to get in touch with a live person in the CLAIMS department. They charged our credit card on file for the replacement windshield ($1600!!) - even though our card was on file with them, they did NOT GIVE US A HEADS UP that the charge would appear. They claimed they would help us work with our insurance company to reclaim some of the money and BUT THEY DIDN'T. It took WEEKS for them to send me the copy of the invoice to send to my insurance company. They may be fine to rent through IF YOU DON'T HAVE TO FILE A CLAIM, but if you do, get ready to invest alot of time into an excruciating process.
reviewCompanyResponse:

reviewTitle: Easy to use and responsive to any…
reviewDescription: Easy to use and responsive to any questions I have.

This is a review from a host experience. First of all, I will say that Outdoorsy's response team is extremely fast and I've never had an issue with connecting with support via chat or phone instantly. The representatives have always been kind and worked within their capacity.

However, I do have a serious issue with how much Outdoorsy makes out of their hosts. They take an average of 25% (including minute processing fees afterwards, which in my opinion.. if you're taking 20% and above at least don't charge processing fees for minute fees out or principle). 

To me, this is an absurd amount. Much more than other rental platforms. How much Outdoorsy takes decreases if you make more money with them. Which to me is also odd, because as a rental listing company, you'd imagine that they want to play power in numbers so the more hosts you have, the more the company makes. However, they just charge a lot. At the end of the day, unlike AirBnb... the host has to deal with all the crap that comes with owning a car.. maintenance, fixing, and even paperwork... It's a tech company but they still make users use paperwork as well as sign online which makes 0 sense to me. I never remember signing something on paper on AirBnB. This is especially atrocious because owning a house also comes with all these troublesome maintenance and cleaning, however a house increases in value while a car decreases. 

Having this in mind, I would imagine at least Outdoorsy would match AirBnb's take from their hosts.

Also, I recently had an incident where it was horribly managed and we are now left to fix the damages on our rig without even being able to take from the renter's deposit as they are denying the damages while Departure / Return photos clearly show what happened during the trip.

reviewDescription: Outdoorsy is seamless and easy to use. It’s a great resource for renting your camper.

reviewTitle: Great platform for RV rentals and more
reviewDescription: Great platform for RV rentals and more. I highly recommend

As a host, the real benefit is the insurance blanket outdoorsy has for host/owners. BUT. Once the owner has to file a claim the process takes weeks, if not months, and outdoorsy takes weeks to answer emails and they have never called to make sure everything is ok, or what the process will be. 
I filed a claim two months ago, I am still waiting for the internal outdoorsy adjuster to provide their findings regarding what they will cover.  I paid out of pocket to have this fixed since the reviews matter. Outdoorsy takes 27% of the rental $$$.. Maybe they can upgrade their staff and improve the claim process since owners like myself are the way they make $$$.
reviewCompanyResponse:

Dale R.
Outdoorsy Support

reviewTitle: CLAIMS DEPARTMENT IS TERRIBLE TO WORK WITH

reviewTitle: I’m not happy with the length of time…
reviewDescription: I’m not happy with the length of time claims are taking and the running around and excess time to manage claims since you changed to offering excess only insurance policies that have to be denied by the renters auto insurance before the insurance purchased through outdoorsy kicks in.    I received a denial due to mechanical breakdown and since it broke down in AZ and I am in MO, when I picked up the RV in AZ after work was completed the windshield was cracked, a tire was damaged, a hubcap was missing, the hitch mounted bike rack was missing and other exterior and interior damage and I can’t get an answer as to how to get this taken care of.  We already went through fair claims and was told it is an insurance issue but I don’t have the renters auto insurance to make a claim. I have emailed multiple times per week since June and still no resolution!  Now the renter and I are being told we have to go through fair claims again.
reviewCompanyResponse:

Dale R.

reviewDescription:
I have finished my 3rd rental recently (as a host), and I could not be more pleased. I have needed to utilize Outdoorsy Support numerous times as I was learning to navigate their site, app, financial questions, and how to properly prepare for renting my RV out - probably between 10-15 times. Each time, I felt that I was talking to a real person who attentively listened to my problem and found a solution promptly.
Every single time, the person I was communicating with found a solution - without having to transfer me, put me on a significant hold, or otherwise make me jump through hoops. Outdoorsy has some of the best support I have ever experienced, both on the phone and on their Chat.

I had a lot of worries when I first endeavored to list my RV on a rental platform, and subject it to the treatment of complete strangers. But I have been pleasantly surprised at my experience thus far - my renters have been excellent communicators, attentive listeners when I walk them through my rv, and have evidently treated my RV as if it was their own, prized possession. 

I highly recommend Outdoorsy as a trusted platform to offer personal RVs for rent. As a host, I find their services to be well worth the commission. I sincerely hope they maintain their best quality, which certainly gives them an extreme edge in the market - which is their customer support.

So essentially outdoorsy said we can fix it now, but we don't care you lost out on hundreds of dollars at this point. Then because if the glitch these renters canceled the rental losing me an additional $780. I'll keep my future rentals to not impact booked renters, but once I honor those i'll be leaving the site. Outdoorsy doesn't care about you or your rig at all. As long as they take their cut, which is sizable. Also if you need to charge someone for issues out of YOUR security deposit, they charge a 3% fee. I won't be using their platform moving forward.

reviewTitle: Great job so far, some critiques.
reviewDescription:
Loving the mileage and generator useage calculator function, although I do wish they would patch the pricing indicators to read what currency people are looking at, it's back to showing USD but no "USD" beside the dollar value, so I get a lot of canadian renters asking why my prices are different. 

I also wish it was a bit clearler for renters to leave a review, RVezy kind of forces them to write something where Outdoorsy kind of just lets them not leave anything which can hurt traffic for my page.

I use the 3 major RV rental cites to list my RV and Outdoorsy is by far the best. Bookings from Outdoorsy are just smother and have far less issues. I had one guest on another cite that kept having their booking canceled. After a huge ordeal I had them book through Outdoorsy and they were instantly approved for their trip.
Outdoorsy is excellent when you need help, so quick. Call backs are typically 1-2 minutes. I’ve had several small issues come up and was pleasantly surprised how fast they were resolved.
everything about outdoorsy for hosting is great. I have zero complaints.
Great platform and customer service.
I am confident that my guests are insured and my RV is protected. It's a great way to reduce the net cost of owning an RV.
I’m perfectly satisfied with outdoorsy.

I've been hosting on Outdoorsy for about 4 months now. I could not be more displeased with this website. Here are my complaints:
(1) the app is constantly signing me out and my cohost and asking for a special pass key.... ergo it wastes a lot of my time.
(2) my car was recently damaged and I thought Outdoorsy would be as helpful as possible considering their VERY high premiums for hosts and clients. They told me to contact the clients insurance company which created a circular disaster of about 12 phone calls none of which resulting in my damages getting fixed or insurance money... Following this, I had to settle with my client directly. I told my client to Venmo me the damages which Outdoorsy jumped on immediately and threatened to cancel my account if I did not get reimbursed through the app.... Not only that they wanted to charge me a fee for the transfer of money for my damaged vehicle. 
(3) Fast forward two weeks they ACTUALLY SUSPEND my account because I told the client I was only taking bookings through Turo as Outdoorsy needs to fix their glitches on the website and app; also I'm still waiting on damages reimbursement from Outdoorsy. (based on how much money outdoorsy brings in this should not be a difficult process).   
(4) Just to give you context of how much Outdoorsy charges a client and how much they pay a host: my last potential client was going to pay $1330 for the reservation out of which I was only going to get $770... That's an enormous premium for low quality performance. 

AS OF NOW, I'M STILL WAITING TO RECEIVE MONEY FOR DAMAGES. AND they have the audacity to suspend my account for just telling a client I'm only taking bookings through another app. There must be some law against this.
reviewCompanyResponse:
Hi Giorgio,

I'm so sorry to hear you've had a poor hosting experience due to app issues, a damage claim, and the suspension of your account. I'll be investigating this thoroughly on my end, and I'll reach out to you directly to discuss this further.

reviewTitle: Cool experience for renter, but not worth hosting.
reviewDescription: Cool experience for renters, but not for owners. Their fees are exorbitant. We recently rented our camper for $500 for a 5 day period (our total including cleaning and generator rental). By the time the renter got to checkout, it was $700 (required insurance, taxes and fees.) At this point Outdoorsy has jacked up our rental fee by easily 30-40%. Of course that's not enough...they take 30% of our rental fee too. By the time it's all over, we saw basically see 55-60% of what the renter paid, which is frankly ridiculous given the amount of time required for hand offs and the fact that we own the infrastructure. These added fees seem to be killing our occupancy too... we're one of the lowest cost, and one of the only vintage campers in our area, and despite excellent reviews, we've seen a huge slow-down this summer.
reviewCompanyResponse:

Dale R.

reviewTitle: Watch your reviews and communicate quickly if there is an error.
reviewDescription: UPDATE:  As can happen with technology - a problem can arise that was unintended and that appears to be what happened to me.  For that reason, I am amending my comments because while the system is flawed - the customer service team responded professionally, acknowledged a system flaw, and confirmed that it shouldn't have happened - which is GREAT.  Mistakes happen, and that is understandable. I feel confident that my issue was a glitch and for that reason, and the great communication with Outoorsey, I am revising my star rating from 1 to 4.  PRIOR REVIEW:  I saw an "automated review" on my account today that said, "Faith Lucchesi canceled reservation two days before our trip. Automated review."  While true in part, the guest initiated the cancellation, and to be kind, I AGREED TO REFUND ALL FEES that would have been due because of the cancellation policy. By agreeing to assist a renter, Outdoorsy's automated system told all other renters that I canceled someone's trip without the added fact that I did it at their request and agreed to refund all monies despite the cancellation policy. When I saw the review, many months after the harm was done, I called to discuss the matter, and the review was removed, but the damage was done. I was seriously misportrayed by the platform for more than six months, without my knowledge -and it was THEIR automatic response to the situation that published the review. NOT the guest, not me as host. And it was a lie as stated. The corporate decision to punish hosts who help guests is a shockingly myopic business decision and others should know this when reading reviews or considering becoming a host.
reviewCompanyResponse:
Hi Faith,

I'm so sorry to hear you were penalized due to a mutual cancellation. This is most definitely not what should've happened, and I'll be investigating on my end to find out why this occurred. We absolutely don't intend to punish hosts for providing their guests with a full refund, so your review is quite concerning to me. Your time is valuable, so thank you so much for sharing your feedback and bringing this to my attention. I'll be reaching out to you directly to provide insight on how this unfortunate situation may be prevented in the future. 

Jaime
Outdoorsy Support
